programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: Future Lifeguards
programs.description: Prepare to be a future lifeguard in this free activity. Here, swimmers will continue to develop the skills they need to successfully complete Bronze level courses, which are a requirement to become a lifeguard, as well as be encouraged to apply for City of Toronto recreation employment with the support of City staff.Prerequisite: You must be able to demonstrate 70% completion of the Future Lifeguard screening on week one of the program (Ultra 6/7 swimming ability). Candidates who do not meet this requirement will be referred to other City of Toronto swim programming.

programs.name: Games: Chess
programs.description: From active to interactive and simple to strategic, games are a great way to gain new skills. In chess, a two-player game where game pieces are used to move across the board with the object to checkmate your opponent, you will learn patience, tactics and critical thinking as well as game rules.

programs.name: Gardening with Caregiver
programs.description: This is a great introduction to the garden. Learn how to take care of plants and what makes them grow with hands-on practice and fun activities. A caregiver must actively participate in this activity.

programs.name: Gentle Fit
programs.description: Seeking a class with a more relaxed pace to improve your fitness? Using gentle movements and low-intensity level exercises, workouts will include both  cardio and muscle conditioning options.

programs.name: Guardian Swim 1
programs.description: Swim together to promote confidence and safety in the water. Learn how to enter and exit the pool, go underwater, breathe effectively, and float with assistance. One caregiver per swimmer must participate in the water at all times.

programs.name: Guardian Swim 1
programs.description: Swim together to promote confidence and safety in the water. Learn how to enter and exit the pool, go underwater, breathe effectively, and float with assistance. One caregiver per swimmer must participate in the water at all times.

programs.name: CampTO Explore
programs.description: CampTO Explore is a fun and creative all-day experience. Enjoy games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. Water play or swim days will also be enjoyed, dependent on weather and camp location. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.

programs.name: CampTO Plus Museums: Secret Garden at Spadina Museum
programs.description: Be immersed in the beautiful gardens to learn about nature, plants, wildlife and the history of this land. Using seasonal produce of the kitchen garden, make delicious treats in the kitchen of the historic coach house. Inspired by nature and the beautiful art in the museum, this camp has plenty of arts and crafts for children to unleash their imagination and creativity to express themselves through paint, clay, collage and more.  Campers bring their curiosity and travel back in time to the 1920s to discover the secrets of the house and uncover amazing and surprising history.  In addition to the extensive outdoor space, campers have a cool and comfortable home base in the and make special visits throughout the historic mansion as well.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Arts and Nature
programs.description: This camp combines the study of ecology and art. Campers will have an opportunity to learn about preservation and the identification of plants and animals by exploring the environment. As well, using nature as inspiration, campers will create art projects, from drawings and paintings to relief art and sculpture.In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on arts and nature, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp program.

programs.name: CampTO Plus Museums: Secret Garden at Spadina Museum

Using seasonal produce of the kitchen garden, make delicious treats in the kitchen of the historic coach house. Inspired by nature and the beautiful art in the museum, this camp has plenty of arts and crafts for children to unleash their imagination and creativity to express themselves through paint, clay, collage and more.  Campers bring their curiosity and travel back in time to the 1920s to discover the secrets of the house and uncover amazing and surprising history.  In addition to the extensive outdoor space, campers have a cool and comfortable home base in the and make special visits throughout the historic mansion as well.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Arts and Nature
programs.description: This camp combines the study of ecology and art. Campers will have an opportunity to learn about preservation and the identification of plants and animals by exploring the environment. As well, using nature as inspiration, campers will create art projects, from drawings and paintings to relief art and sculpture.In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on arts and nature, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp program.

programs.name: Future Lifeguards
programs.description: Prepare to be a future lifeguard in this free activity. Here, swimmers will continue to develop the skills they need to successfully complete Bronze level courses, which are a requirement to become a lifeguard, as well as be encouraged to apply for City of Toronto recreation employment with the support of City staff.Prerequisite: You must be able to demonstrate 70% completion of the Future Lifeguard screening on week one of the program (Ultra 6/7 swimming ability). Candidates who do not meet this requirement will be referred to other City of Toronto swim programming.

programs.name: Games: Chess
programs.description: From active to interactive and simple to strategic, games are a great way to gain new skills. In chess, a two-player game where game pieces are used to move across the board with the object to checkmate your opponent, you will learn patience, tactics and critical thinking as well as game rules.

programs.name: Gardening with Caregiver
programs.description: This is a great introduction to the garden. Learn how to take care of plants and what makes them grow with hands-on practice and fun activities. A caregiver must actively participate in this activity.

programs.name: Gentle Fit
programs.description: Seeking a class with a more relaxed pace to improve your fitness? Using gentle movements and low-intensity level exercises, workouts will include both  cardio and muscle conditioning options.

programs.name: Guardian Swim 1
programs.description: Swim together to promote confidence and safety in the water. Learn how to enter and exit the pool, go underwater, breathe effectively, and float with assistance. One caregiver per swimmer must participate in the water at all times.

programs.name: Guardian Swim 1

programs.name: Future Lifeguards
programs.description: Prepare to be a future lifeguard in this free activity. Here, swimmers will continue to develop the skills they need to successfully complete Bronze level courses, which are a requirement to become a lifeguard, as well as be encouraged to apply for City of Toronto recreation employment with the support of City staff.Prerequisite: You must be able to demonstrate 70% completion of the Future Lifeguard screening on week one of the program (Ultra 6/7 swimming ability). Candidates who do not meet this requirement will be referred to other City of Toronto swim programming.

programs.name: Games: Chess
programs.description: From active to interactive and simple to strategic, games are a great way to gain new skills. In chess, a two-player game where game pieces are used to move across the board with the object to checkmate your opponent, you will learn patience, tactics and critical thinking as well as game rules.

programs.name: Gardening with Caregiver
programs.description: This is a great introduction to the garden. Learn how to take care of plants and what makes them grow with hands-on practice and fun activities. A caregiver must actively participate in this activity.

programs.name: Gentle Fit
programs.description: Seeking a class with a more relaxed pace to improve your fitness? Using gentle movements and low-intensity level exercises, workouts will include both  cardio and muscle conditioning options.

programs.name: Guardian Swim 1
programs.description: Swim together to promote confidence and safety in the water. Learn how to enter and exit the pool, go underwater, breathe effectively, and float with assistance. One caregiver per swimmer must participate in the water at all times.

programs.name: CampTO Plus Museums: Secret Garden at Spadina Museum
programs.description: Be immersed in the beautiful gardens to learn about nature, plants, wildlife and the history of this land. Using seasonal produce of the kitchen garden, make delicious treats in the kitchen of the historic coach house. Inspired by nature and the beautiful art in the museum, this camp has plenty of arts and crafts for children to unleash their imagination and creativity to express themselves through paint, clay, collage and more.  Campers bring their curiosity and travel back in time to the 1920s to discover the secrets of the house and uncover amazing and surprising history.  In addition to the extensive outdoor space, campers have a cool and comfortable home base in the and make special visits throughout the historic mansion as well.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: CampTO Plus Museums: Secret Garden at Spadina Museum
programs.description: Be immersed in the beautiful gardens to learn about nature, plants, wildlife and the history of this land. Using seasonal produce of the kitchen garden, make delicious treats in the kitchen of the historic coach house. Inspired by nature and the beautiful art in the museum, this camp has plenty of arts and crafts for children to unleash their imagination and creativity to express themselves through paint, clay, collage and more.  Campers bring their curiosity and travel back in time to the 1920s to discover the secrets of the house and uncover amazing and surprising history.  In addition to the extensive outdoor space, campers have a cool and comfortable home base in the and make special visits throughout the historic mansion as well.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Arts and Nature
programs.description: This camp combines the study of ecology and art. Campers will have an opportunity to learn about preservation and the identification of plants and animals by exploring the environment. As well, using nature as inspiration, campers will create art projects, from drawings and paintings to relief art and sculpture.In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on arts and nature, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp program.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: SPLASH Swim Team
programs.description: Expand your swimming skills by practicing your strokes in a team-based environment and showcase your skills during competitive swim meets. Prerequisite: Must be able to swim at least two continuous pool lengths (approximately 50 metres) of unassisted front swim and demonstrate ability to take breaths. IMPORTANT: The City of Toronto is dedicated to ensuring the safety of all individuals who participate in its activities and services. This program requires the review of Ontario's Concussion Awareness Resources and the City of Toronto's Concussion Code of Conduct. A signature of acknowledgment is required for participants under the age of 26, or parents/caregivers of participants under the age of 18, on the first day of program participation.Visit www.toronto.ca/concussionsafety for more information on concussion safety in our sports and recreation programs.

programs.name: CampTO Plus Museums: Secret Garden at Spadina Museum
programs.description: Be immersed in the beautiful gardens to learn about nature, plants, wildlife and the history of this land. Using seasonal produce of the kitchen garden, make delicious treats in the kitchen of the historic coach house. Inspired by nature and the beautiful art in the museum, this camp has plenty of arts and crafts for children to unleash their imagination and creativity to express themselves through paint, clay, collage and more.  Campers bring their curiosity and travel back in time to the 1920s to discover the secrets of the house and uncover amazing and surprising history.  In addition to the extensive outdoor space, campers have a cool and comfortable home base in the and make special visits throughout the historic mansion as well.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Arts and Nature
programs.description: This camp combines the study of ecology and art. Campers will have an opportunity to learn about preservation and the identification of plants and animals by exploring the environment. As well, using nature as inspiration, campers will create art projects, from drawings and paintings to relief art and sculpture.In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on arts and nature, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp program.

programs.name: Soccer: Instructional with Caregiver
programs.description: Instructional sports activities are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In this soccer activity, you will learn basics like kicking, passing and heading as well as game rules through drills, practice and play. A caregiver must actively participate in this activity.

programs.name: SPLASH Swim Team
programs.description: Expand your swimming skills by practicing your strokes in a team-based environment and showcase your skills during competitive swim meets. Prerequisite: Must be able to swim at least two continuous pool lengths (approximately 50 metres) of unassisted front swim and demonstrate ability to take breaths. IMPORTANT: The City of Toronto is dedicated to ensuring the safety of all individuals who participate in its activities and services. This program requires the review of Ontario's Concussion Awareness Resources and the City of Toronto's Concussion Code of Conduct. A signature of acknowledgment is required for participants under the age of 26, or parents/caregivers of participants under the age of 18, on the first day of program participation.Visit www.toronto.ca/concussionsafety for more information on concussion safety in our sports and recreation programs.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 5
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front dive, forward roll and front crawl. Learn back crawl, eggbeater kick and elementary backstroke. Work towards a continuous swim of 75 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 4.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 5 - Small Group
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front dive, forward roll and front crawl. Learn back crawl, eggbeater kick and elementary backstroke. Work towards a continuous swim of 75 metres. Class size is limited to four participants.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 4.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 5 - Small Group
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front dive, forward roll and front crawl. Learn back crawl, eggbeater kick and elementary backstroke. Work towards a continuous swim of 75 metres. Class size is limited to four participants.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 4.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 5 (Girls)
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front dive, forward roll and front crawl. Learn back crawl, eggbeater kick and elementary backstroke. Work towards a continuous swim of 75 metres. This activity is for all girls.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 4.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 7
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop surface dives, eggbeater kick, front crawl, back crawl, elementary backstroke and breaststroke. Learn shallow dives into deep water and treading water while fully clothed. Work towards a continuous swim of 200 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 6.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 7 - Small Group
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop surface dives, eggbeater kick, front crawl, back crawl, elementary backstroke and breaststroke. Learn shallow dives into deep water and treading water while fully clothed. Work towards a continuous swim of 200 metres. Class size is limited to four participants.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 6.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 8
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop surface dives with underwater swimming, interval training, object support and all strokes. Work towards a continuous swim of 300 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 7.

programs.name: Tae Kwon Do with Belts
programs.description: This activity introduces you to martial arts.  Here you will develop fundamental martial art skills, simple sparring skills, tactical concepts, show willingness to engage in a variety of sport activities and have confidence to try new skills.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: CampTO Plus Museums: Secret Garden at Spadina Museum
programs.description: Be immersed in the beautiful gardens to learn about nature, plants, wildlife and the history of this land. Using seasonal produce of the kitchen garden, make delicious treats in the kitchen of the historic coach house. Inspired by nature and the beautiful art in the museum, this camp has plenty of arts and crafts for children to unleash their imagination and creativity to express themselves through paint, clay, collage and more.  Campers bring their curiosity and travel back in time to the 1920s to discover the secrets of the house and uncover amazing and surprising history.  In addition to the extensive outdoor space, campers have a cool and comfortable home base in the and make special visits throughout the historic mansion as well.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Arts and Nature
programs.description: This camp combines the study of ecology and art. Campers will have an opportunity to learn about preservation and the identification of plants and animals by exploring the environment. As well, using nature as inspiration, campers will create art projects, from drawings and paintings to relief art and sculpture.In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on arts and nature, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp program.

To register contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Adapted CampTO Explore: Iron Butterflies
programs.description: Adapted CampTO Iron Butterflies is a fun and creative camp experience for campers with a physical disability that requires support and personal care. This camp provides an opportunity to make friends when participating in arts, games and sports activities while reducing barriers. A workshop, local trip or special guest will add to the excitement.Personal support workers are hired to provide care.Participants must have a current AIS membership to register for this program. For more information call 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca

programs.description: Adapted CampTO Iron Butterflies is a fun and creative camp experience for campers with a physical disability that requires support and personal care. programs.name: Adapted Cooking
programs.description: Healthy eating begins with good cooking. Come learn the basics of cooking and how to prepare delicious and nutritious meals on a budget. You will handle all aspects of food preparation from writing a menu, shopping and handling money, as well as using public transport to completing a recipe you can enjoy.This activity is designed for individuals with an intellectual disability.The City of Toronto and its staff cannot guarantee that the foods or supplies within its cooking programs are free of allergens. Notify staff at your location of any food allergies or dietary restrictions you may have.Participants must have a current AIS membership, to register contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Adapted Counsellor in Training
programs.description: Becoming a camp counsellor is a once-in-a-lifetime experience. In this camp, you will learn about program planning and working with campers in an interactive environment. You will also get the opportunity to do a supervised practical placement in a City of Toronto summer camp and receive a certificate upon completion.As this is a registered activity, participants will not receive credit for volunteer hours. For more information on volunteer opportunities, contact your local community centre.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

To register contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Adapted Ultra Swim - Advanced
programs.description: Swim at your own pace. You will begin to explore more advanced swim skills such as surface dives, stride jump, treading skills and all strokes and kicks. You will continue to develop your lifesaving and self rescue techniques. Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Adapted Ultra Swim - Intermediate. Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or aiscw@toronto.ca. Please Note: External support persons attending with you shall enter the water as required and can attend at no charge.

programs.name: Adapted Ultra Swim - Beginner
programs.description: Swim at your own pace. Discover water movements through sensory cues such as look, listen and feel and practice fun ways to enter and exit the pool to gain comfort in the water. Learn essential swimming skills such as floating, kicking, and gliding and understand the importance of water safety and how to be safe in and around water environments. Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or aiscw@toronto.ca. Please Note: External support persons attending with you shall enter water as required and can attend at no charge.

programs.name: Adapted Ultra Swim - Intermediate
programs.description: Swim at your own pace. While exploring and gaining independence at this level, you will learn the mechanics of front crawl, back crawl and diving as you continue to enhance your deep-water techniques, survival and self-rescue skills. Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Adapted Ultra Swim - Beginner. Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or aiscw@toronto.ca. Please Note: External support persons attending with you shall enter the water as required and can attend at no charge.

programs.name: Adapted Ultra Swim - Intermediate
programs.description: Swim at your own pace. While exploring and gaining independence at this level, you will learn the mechanics of front crawl, back crawl and diving as you continue to enhance your deep-water techniques, survival and self-rescue skills. Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Adapted Ultra Swim - Beginner. Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or aiscw@toronto.ca. Please Note: External support persons attending with you shall enter the water as required and can attend at no charge.

programs.name: Adult Swim 1
programs.description: Build confidence in the water one stroke at a time. Learn front and back swim, front, side and back glides, going underwater, breathing effectively and staying afloat in deep water. Work towards a continuous swim of 25 to 50 metres.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: Aquatic Fitness: Deep
programs.description: A deep water workout that includes cardio muscle conditioning, flexibility and strength building that uses belts or noodles to assist with performance during the class. Participants must be comfortable in deep water and be able to complete a facility swim test.It is free to reserve a spot in this activity, fees may apply at the time of check in. Please arrive 10 minutes before your scheduled start time to check in with staff and pay for this swim. If you do not arrive within 5 minutes of your start time, your spot will be given to someone in the walk-in line. A printed receipt or a mobile screenshot is required at check in.Participants are permitted to bring their own personal equipment.To cancel, visit your online account, speak with community centre staff, or contact Client Services at 416-396-7378 during regular business hours.

programs.name: Badminton: Instructional
programs.description: Instructional sports activities are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In badminton, a racquet sport that focuses on hand-eye coordination and has you using your racquet to rally for points with a ‘birdie’ you will learn basics like grip, serve and swing, as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Basketball: Recreational
programs.description: Develop skills through play with recreational basketball. Players of all levels can register to join the fun and participate in structured scrimmages or small-sided games where staff will ensure rules are followed and participation is balanced. There is no formal instruction.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Drama
programs.description: Step into the spotlight and take part in the mysteries of the theatre through drama coaching, set designs and dramatic performances. Learn and practice drama techniques like improv and role-playing, voice projection, expression and script interpretation via skits and exercises. In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing and fine-tuning your drama skills, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Drawing and Sketching
programs.description: Bring your imagination to life with pen and paper. Campers will learn the fundamentals of drawing and sketching with a focus on developing skills such as perspective and shading using values and tones. In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing and fine-tuning your drawing and sketching skills, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp program.
programs.location: Joseph J. Piccininni Cmty Rec Centre

Join us for co-operative games, arts, sports, basic cooking, swimming, physical activity workshops, community walks and more.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register, contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.
